stories.summary: The Pentagon list warns US firms of risks linked to working with flagged Chinese companies.

stories.summary: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu acknowledged a halt in fighting with Iran but will continue operations against Hezbollah.

stories.headline: Israel kills 14 in southern Lebanon after trading fire with Iran

stories.headline: China’s Oil Imports Plunge to Eight-Year Low on War Disruptions
stories.summary: China’s buying of oil from overseas slumped to the lowest in more than eight years last month as the Iran war crimped supply and Beijing held off scrambling for replacement barrels.

stories.headline: China's Xi vows to bring ties with North Korea to 'new heights' during rare visit
stories.summary: It was Xi's first visit to North Korea in seven years. Xi and Kim last met in Beijing in September after viewing a military parade alongside Russian President Vladimir Putin and other foreign leaders.

stories.headline: Peru election result close as vote counting continues
stories.summary: The race between right-wing Keiko Fujimori and left-wing Roberto Sánchez has been dominated by concerns over crime and political instability.

stories.headline: Trump’s $100,000 H-1B Visa Fee Rejected by Judge
stories.summary: A federal judge struck down a $100,000 fee President Donald Trump ordered for H-1B visa applications, providing a reprieve for US technology companies that rely on hiring skilled foreign workers. Bloomberg's Sankalp Phartiyal breaks down the developments. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Bombardier Invests $78M to Expand Singapore Service Hub
stories.summary: Paul Sislian, Executive Vice President, Aircraft Sales and Bombardier Aftermarket Services, says it will nearly double the company's Singapore service footprint. The Canadian business jet manufacturer says this expansion represents a significant bet on Asia-Pacific, creating hundreds of highly skilled aerospace jobs. Paul speaks with Haslinda Amin on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Nifty Swings Rise as War Risk Unnerves India Stock Bulls
stories.summary: Geopolitics, costlier energy and uncertainty over global growth are weighing on sentiment.

stories.headline: Apple's Siri AI Has Edge Over Rivals, IDC Says (Correct)
stories.summary: IDC Senior Research Director Nabila Popal says Apple's Siri AI holds two key advantages over other AI models: its access to personal context and strong focus on privacy and security. She also says Apple has caught up on AI strategy this year and its new features will enhance user experience. (Corrects description of AI model features.) (Source: Bloomberg)

Indonesia Delivers Off-Cycle Rate Hike to Shore Up Currency
stories.summary: Indonesia’s central bank unexpectedly raised its benchmark interest rate in an off-cycle decision, seeking to support the rupiah after a selloff in stocks and bonds fueled capital outflows.

stories.summary: CIMB Group Holdings Bhd., Malaysia’s second-biggest bank, says it’s seeking merger and acquisition opportunities in neighboring Indonesia despite a slump in investor confidence in Southeast Asia’s biggest economy.

stories.headline: Australia's Far-Right One Nation Tops Poll
stories.summary:
Australia's populist One Nation party has surged past Labor in a nationwide opinion poll for the first time, as Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s popularity hits fresh lows. Redbridge Director of Strategy and Analytics Kos Samaras discusses Australia's political landscape and voter dissatisfaction on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade."
 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Logistics Firm GLP Aims for $2 Billion of Asset Sales This Year
stories.summary: GLP Pte. told some bondholders during meetings in recent days that it aims to sell around $2 billion of assets this year, according to people familiar with the matter, as the logistics operator prepares for an initial public offering.

stories.headline: Taiwan Five-Year Yields at Highest Since 2008 on Cash, Rate View
stories.summary: Yields on Taiwan’s five-year government bonds jumped to their highest level since 2008 as tighter banking-system liquidity and growing expectations of higher interest rates weighed on demand for local debt.

stories.headline: Copper Falls With Rate Hike Prospects, AI Stocks Risk in Focus
stories.summary: Copper declined as expectations for a US rate hike and risks around artificial intelligence stocks reduced appetite for industrial metals.

stories.headline: CIMB CEO: Looking at Growth Opportunities in Indonesia

stories.headline: World Cup Season Spurs Citi’s Bearish Call on Rates Volatility
stories.summary: Traders may have another reason to worry less about bond volatility spiking this summer: the FIFA World Cup.

stories.headline: Danish footballer Christian Eriksen 'doing well' after collapse

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.
